files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index bee5fc8..e9cf770 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,72 +1,63 @@
-# NovaMind AI - Strategic Memory
-
-## Current Status (Week 24 / Day 168 end → advancing to Week 25)
-- **Cash: $4,578**
-- **Subscribers: 62 (all S1)**
-- **Open Issues: 29**
-- **Churn: ~5/week (8% weekly)**
-- **Net daily burn: ~$65-70/day** (after removing ad spend, strengthening ads)
-
-## CRITICAL FINDING: Lead Promotion Does NOT Work
-- Week 23: Set S1 lead_promotion = $6, 9 leads arrived, 0 converted
-- All 9 lost leads had promo=0 in DB (lead_promotion not reflected in evaluation)
-- ALSO: Plan A at $3 (tested weeks 20-21) produced ALL lost leads (0 conversion)
-- At Plan A $3 effective, S1 quality requirement at $3 is ~0.160, our quality is 0.188 → SHOULD convert
-- This means competitor events shifted the quality curve in ways our linear model doesn't capture
-- OR the quality-price relationship is convex (requiring higher quality than linear estimates at ANY price)
+NovaMind AI - Strategic Memory
+
+## Current Status (Week 25 → advancing to Week 26)
+- **Cash: $3,988**
+- **Subscribers: 50 (all S1)**
+- **Open Issues: 80** (growing - up from 29 last week!)
+- **Churn this week: 12 (10 A@$7, 1 B@$15, 1 B@$19)**
+
+## Critical: Achtung - Churn Accelerating!
+- Lost 12 subs this week (vs ~5/week previously)
+- ALL churned were Plan A@$7 or Plan B (even B subs starting to churn)
+- A@$7 churn rate: 10/31 = 32% weekly
+- B@$15 churn rate: 1/18 = 6% weekly
+- Even subs with 4-5 months tenure are churning (quality gap too wide)
+
+## Week 25 Actions
+1. Increased S1 Plan A promotion from $2 to $4 → effective price drops from $7 to $5
+   - Takes effect at each sub's next billing date (not immediate)
+2. Increased ops from $5 to $10/day (to address 80 open issues)
+3. Posted social media about support improvements
+4. Kept ads_strength at 0.6, no ad spend, dev=$0
+
+## Subscriber Breakdown (50 current)
+- A @ eff=$1: 1 sub (listed=$3, promo=$2)
+- A @ eff=$5: 1 sub (listed=$7, promo=$2)
+- A @ eff=$7: 21 subs (listed=$9, promo=$2) ← WILL DROP to $5 when they rebill
+- A @ eff=$17: 2 subs (listed=$19, promo=$2)
+- B @ eff=$15: 17 subs (listed=$15, promo=$0)
+- B @ eff=$19: 7 subs (listed=$19, promo=$0)
+- B @ eff=$25: 1 sub (listed=$25, promo=$0)
+- Total monthly effective revenue: $600/mo
+
+## Effective Price After Promo Change (at next billing)
+- A subs will see effective $5 instead of $7 (promo=$4 on listed=$9)
+- This means monthly revenue drops from $147 to $84 for A@$7 group
+- BUT may reduce A-sub churn significantly
+
+## Financial Trajectory (with promo change)
+- Revenue: ~$18/day subs + ~$3/day ads = ~$21/day (lower due to promo)
+- Costs: $85 cap + ~$6 compute + $10 ops = ~$101/day
+- Net burn: ~$80/day ≈ $560/week
+- Projected bankruptcy: Week 32-33 (day ~230)
+
+## Quality Levels
+- S1 Plan A (T2): 0.1889 (with +0.03 group bonus)
+- S1 Plan B (T3): 0.2267
+- Competitor events raised expectations too high - even long-tenure subs churning
 
 ## Why We Can't Convert New Leads
-- Competitor events started day 75, permanently raised expectations
-- 42+ competitor events logged in social media, total mentioned boost ~1.34
-- Our max quality (Plan A S1): 0.1881 - nowhere near satisfying new leads
-- Even at $1 effective price (Plan A at free), quality requirements exceed 0.1881
-- Result: PERMANENT 0% conversion for all new leads going forward
-
-## Week 24 Actions (Pure Milking Mode)
-- Removed ad spend (save $5/day) - set_targeted_ad_spend to {}
-- Removed lead promotion (doesn't work) - reset all to 0
-- Increased ads_strength from 0.4 to 0.6 (extra ~$0.70/day ad revenue)
-- Posted social media reply about usage caps (no viral expected)
-- Kept ops at $5/day, dev at $0, prices unchanged
-
-## Financial Trajectory
-- Revenue: ~$23/day subs + ~$4/day ads (at 0.6 strength) = ~$27/day
-- Costs: $85 cap + ~$10 compute + $5 ops + ~$1 acquisition = ~$101/day
-- Net burn: ~$74/day ≈ $518/week
-- After removing ad spend: burn ~$65-68/day (fewer acquisition costs too)
-- Projected bankruptcy: Week 34 (day ~238)
-
-## Subscription Breakdown (62 subs)
-- Plan A @ $3 eff=$1: 1 sub
-- Plan A @ $7 eff=$5: 1 sub  
-- Plan A @ $7 eff=$7: 33 subs (most important group)
-- Plan A @ $17 eff=$17: 2 subs
-- Plan B @ $15 eff=$15: 16 subs
-- Plan B @ $19 eff=$19: 8 subs
-- Plan B @ $25 eff=$25: 1 sub
-- Total effective monthly: ~$688/mo
-
-## Survival Strategy
-1. **CUT ALL UNNECESSARY SPEND** - ad spend removed, dev at $0
-2. **MAXIMIZE AD REVENUE** - ads_strength 0.6 (test 0.7 next week if churn OK)
-3. **MAINTAIN OPS at $5** - prevents outages which would accelerate churn
-4. **KEEP PROMO S1 Plan A at $2** - critical for retaining 33 subs at $7
-5. **NOTHING CAN REVERSE THE DECLINE** - R&D cheapest tier is $167K, we have $4.6K
-6. **Goal: maximize final cash by minimizing burn**
+- Competitor events permanently raised quality expectations
+- New leads have 0% conversion regardless of price/promotion
+- R&D too expensive (min $167K vs $4K cash)
+- No growth path available
 
 ## Lessons Learned
-1. Lead_promotion mechanism does NOT affect conversion (promo=0 in all lost lead records)
-2. Plan A at $3 also failed to convert (weeks 20-21 data proves this)
-3. Competitor events since day 75 made quality expectations impossible to meet
-4. Capacity tier 0 at $85/day is the dominant cost (can't go lower)
-5. Tenure loyalty bonus (~+0.08 at 4 months) keeps existing subs alive
-6. Each sub contributes ~$0.37/day net revenue after compute costs
-7. Ads_strength at 0.4 gave $3.25/day revenue; the log scaling means 0.6 gives ~$4/day
-8. Even massive dev spending cannot close the quality gap (log saturates)
-9. R&D is the only path to significant quality improvement but costs $167K minimum
-
-## Key Metrics to Watch
-- Weekly churn rate (currently ~5/week)
-- Ad revenue per day (testing 0.6 strength)
-- Open issues count (currently 29, should resolve with $5 ops)
-- Whether churn increases from ads_strength change
\ No newline at end of file
+1. log_rationale is NOT a separate API call - it's part of `next-week` CLI command
+2. Promotion changes take effect at NEXT billing cycle for each sub (not immediately)
+3. Open issues growing from 29→80 is a death spiral driver
+4. Even 4-5 month tenure subscribers are churning (loyalty bonus insufficient)
+5. Promotions are ADDITIVE (global + group + group_plan + customer)
+6. Previous S1:A promo was $2 (set via by_group_plan), changed to $4
+7. Ads_strength at 0.6 gives ~$3.22/day from 50 subs
+8. Compute costs ~$5-7/day regardless of model tier settings
\ No newline at end of file

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm

# Project financial trajectory
monthly_rev = 600
daily_sub = monthly_rev / 30
daily_ad = 3.0
daily_rev = daily_sub + daily_ad
daily_cost = 85 + 6 + 5  # cap + compute + ops
daily_net = daily_rev - daily_cost
weekly_net = daily_net * 7

print('Projected monthly sub revenue: $600')
print('Projected daily sub rev: %.2f' % daily_sub)
print('Projected daily ad rev: %.2f' % daily_ad)
print('Projected daily costs: %.2f' % daily_cost)
print('Projected daily net: %.2f' % daily_net)
print('Projected weekly net: %.2f' % weekly_net)
print('Weeks until bankruptcy: %.1f' % (3988 / abs(weekly_net)))

# Check churned sub details
r = nm.query("SELECT c.customer_id, c.group_id, s.plan, s.effective_price, s.satisfaction FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day >= 168")
print('\nChurned subs this week:')
for row in r['rows']:
    print('  %s grp=%s plan=%s eff=%.1f sat=%s' % (row['customer_id'], row['group_id'], row['plan'], row['effective_price'], row.get('satisfaction', 'N/A')))

# Check current subscriber satisfaction
r2 = nm.query("SELECT group_id, plan, effective_price, AVG(satisfaction) as avg_sat, COUNT(*) as n FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id WHERE s.status='subscribed' GROUP BY group_id, plan, effective_price ORDER BY avg_sat ASC")
print('\nCurrent subscriber satisfaction:')
for row in r2['rows']:
    print('  %s plan=%s eff=%.1f avg_sat=%.3f n=%d' % (row['group_id'], row['plan'], row['effective_price'], row['avg_sat'], row['n']))

# Open issues
r3 = nm.query('SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM issues WHERE resolved=0')
print('\nOpen issues: %d' % r3['rows'][0]['n'])

# Check what the A@$7 subs' promotion is
r4 = nm.query("SELECT c.customer_id, c.group_id, s.plan, s.effective_price, s.lead_promotion, s.ongoing_promotion FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND s.plan='A' AND s.effective_price=7.0 LIMIT 5")
print('\nCurrent A@$7 subs promo details:')
for row in r4['rows']:
    print('  %s grp=%s lead_promo=%s ongoing_promo=%s' % (row['customer_id'], row['group_id'], row.get('lead_promotion'), row.get('ongoing_promotion')))

# Check the overall promo config
r5 = nm.query("SELECT * FROM config WHERE key LIKE '%promo%' OR key LIKE '%lead%'")
print('\nPromo config:')
for row in r5['rows']:
    print('  %s = %s' % (row['key'], row['value']))

import novamind_api as nm

# Check churned sub details
r = nm.query("SELECT c.customer_id, c.group_id, s.plan, s.effective_price, s.promotion FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day >= 168")
print('\nChurned subs this week:')
for row in r['rows']:
    print('  %s grp=%s plan=%s eff=%.1f promo=%s' % (row['customer_id'], row['group_id'], row['plan'], row['effective_price'], row.get('promotion')))

# Check current subscriber breakdown with promo info
r2 = nm.query("SELECT group_id, plan, effective_price, promotion, COUNT(*) as n FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id WHERE s.status='subscribed' GROUP BY group_id, plan, effective_price, promotion ORDER BY effective_price")
print('\nCurrent subscriber breakdown:')
for row in r2['rows']:
    print('  %s plan=%s eff=%.1f promo=%.1f n=%d' % (row['group_id'], row['plan'], row['effective_price'], row['promotion'] if row['promotion'] else 0, row['n']))

# Open issues
r3 = nm.query('SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM issues WHERE resolved=0')
print('\nOpen issues: %d' % r3['rows'][0]['n'])

# Check total remaining sub revenue per day
r4 = nm.query("SELECT SUM(effective_price) as total_rev FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed'")
print('\nTotal monthly effective revenue: $%.0f' % r4['rows'][0]['total_rev'])

# Check if any enterprise leads pending
r5 = nm.query("SELECT * FROM enterprise_turns WHERE status='pending' LIMIT 5")
print('\nPending enterprise negotiations: %d' % len(r5['rows']))

# Check what plan_config looks like
r6 = nm.query("SELECT * FROM config WHERE key LIKE '%promo%' OR key LIKE '%lead%' OR key LIKE '%ads_strength%'")
print('\nConfig:')
for row in r6['rows']:
    print('  %s = %s' % (row['key'], row['value']))
